name: Straight and Level
description: 60-second cruise at 10,000 ft. Altitude must hold within ±50 ft.

name: Climb
description: 120-second climb from 5,000 ft. Must reach at least 8,000 ft.

name: Descent
description: 90-second descent from 10,000 ft. Must lose at least 1,000 ft.

name: Turn Left
description: 30-second standard-rate left turn from heading 090. Must turn ≥30°.

criteria.description: Heading turned left by at least 30 degrees (≤060°)

name: Turn Right
description: 30-second standard-rate right turn from heading 000. Must turn ≥30°.

criteria.description: Heading turned right by at least 30 degrees (≥030°)

name: Combined Climb + Turn
description: 60-second combined climb and left turn. Must gain 1,000 ft and turn 15°.

criteria.description: Heading changed by at least 15 degrees left

name: Extended Cruise (FL350)
description: 10-minute cruise at FL350. Altitude and heading must remain stable.

name: Stuck Altimeter During Climb
description: Altimeter freezes at initial reading for 20s mid-climb. Aircraft must still gain altitude despite faulty sensor.
fault_description: AltitudeSensor STUCK fault: t=10s–30s. Sensor returns the value captured at fault onset.

criteria.description: True altitude gained ≥500ft despite stuck altimeter
True altitude did not exceed 15000ft (no runaway climb)

name: Airspeed Spike — Pitot Anomaly
description: A single +150kt spike in reported airspeed at t=5s simulates a pitot tube transient. True airspeed unaffected.
fault_description: AirspeedSensor SPIKE fault: t=5s, magnitude=+150kts. Sensor reports 390+kts for one sample, then returns to normal.

criteria.description: True airspeed returned to near-normal (200–280kts) after spike
Altitude stable — no upset from spike (±300ft of 10000ft)

name: Heading Dropout During Turn
description: Heading sensor drops to 0° for 10s mid-turn. Aircraft continues turning; true heading diverges from sensor.
fault_description: HeadingSensor DROPOUT fault: t=5s–15s. Sensor returns 0° (null) for the full fault window.

criteria.description: True heading turned left ≥20° despite sensor dropout

name: Altimeter Drift — Baro Error
description: Altimeter accumulates +800ft error over 30s simulating an incorrect barometric pressure setting at cruise altitude.
fault_description: AltitudeSensor DRIFT fault: t=10s–40s, magnitude=+800ft. Reported altitude grows linearly above truth during fault window.

criteria.description: True altitude stable at cruise (within ±100ft of 35000ft at end)
